value.text: The world browser may traverse and project an admitted world, but it cannot mutate world authority
Phase 100 is headless and does not add DOM Electron Playwright or real browser runtime
A world browser is a projection navigator: it reveals admitted world structure without becoming the world's authority
No browser action mutates admitted identity
Inspector paths are projections and not authority

Proposals are non-authoritative until admitted by declaration and receipts
Composer packages renderers raw binary QEMU and closure carriers remain witnesses or projections
An interjection is a projected overlay on an admitted world, not a rewrite of the world's authority
Overlay projection never rewrites admitted world identity

Branch snapshots are projection-only and not canonical history

The live renderer may animate and display an admitted world, but it cannot become the world

No renderer action mutates admitted identity

Sender and transport labels do not create world authority
Phase 106 makes admitted world histories portable before it makes them networked
Phase 106 adds no live peer transport or networking behavior
A peer may offer world history, but only local receipt verification admits it

An offer is not admission
Unknown peers reject unless explicitly declared
Phase 107 lets peers exchange evidence without becoming evidence themselves
Phase 107 models peer exchange without live transport routing pubsub or socket behavior
A subscription may request world updates, but only verified exchanged packages become locally admitted history

Filter match is not admission

Phase 108 adds no live pubsub sockets routing subscriptions over network or transport runtime
Availability may guide repair, but availability is not admissibility
Availability is not authority
Availability match is not admission
Candidate supplier is not trust
Repairable is not repaired

A checkpoint may accelerate replay, but it cannot replace replay verification
Checkpoint is not authority
Checkpoint summary is not replay admission
Checkpoint resume does not bypass verification
Stored accepted status alone cannot admit snapshots
Phase 111 gives replay memory not replay authority
Phase 111 adds no live sockets file watchers pubsub daemons routing or network runtime
Compaction may reduce transport evidence size, but it cannot erase the receipts needed to verify history
Compaction bundle is not authority
Compaction bundle is not replay admission

Compaction does not replace replay verification
Stored accepted status alone cannot admit snapshots

Repair may restore evidence, but only verification restores admissibility
Repair request is not authority
Repair payload is not admission
Peer supplying repair is not authority
Repair may restore evidence but only verification restores admissibility
Phase 113 makes transport evidence survivable without making repair trustworthy
Phase 113 adds no live peer repair requests sockets routing or network runtime
A transport log may replay offers, but only receipt verification can replay admission
Transport log is not authority
Replay entry is not admission
Stored processing is not trusted; replay recomputes Phase 107 Phase 106 and Phase 108 verification
A transport log may replay offers but only receipt verification can replay admission
Phase 110 adds no live sockets file watchers pubsub daemons routing or network runtime
A scheduler may choose what to request next, but scheduling is not trust and delivery is not admission
Scheduler is not authority
Request plan is not admission
Supplier priority is not trust
Delivery success is not receipt validity
Retry policy is not a verification bypass
Corrupt evidence must be requested before it is treated as available
Phase 115 lets the node decide what evidence to ask for next without trusting the request the supplier or the delivery path
World history is not overwritten; it is a receipt-linked chain of admitted declarations
World history is not overwritten; it is a receipt-linked chain of admitted declarations
Phase 104 gives the world memory without smuggling mutation into the present: history is a receipt-linked graph of admitted declarations
Replay follows admitted history edges, not overlay projections
Branching from the same old identity is allowed when each branch has an admitted edge receipt
OMI is a constitutional system for admissible differentiation through oriented boundaries
CONS is the minimal oriented boundary through which admissible differentiation becomes projectable
() is a closed admissible null
()! is explicitly differentiated null
0x20 is the omicron threshold
S-P-O-M is an incidence frame over a boundary
A sparse hole is a declared admissible void
A chunk receipt is a boundary witness
Projection is the observable rendering of differentiated structure

Phase 88 gives OMI peers a transport vocabulary without trusting transport as identity
External adapters are capability bindings over OMI declarations, not replacement authorities
Transport surfaces do not define identity
Device surfaces do not become declarations
Adapter bindings reference declared capabilities, not raw OS permissions
Runtime effects over adapters must emit receipts
Phase 88 declares adapter vocabulary and does not open transports or devices
